result.status_detail: DIAGNOSTIC CAPTURED - the root cause of APF warning rendering failure is identified. DevTools Network tab shows TWO chat?biz_id requests for this prompt: (1) First: 'Fetch request failed' CORS error, size 0.0 kB, time 1.90s ??this is the APF-blocked request. (2) Second (retry by qianwen): 200 h2, size 32.2 kB, time 14.9s ??this SUCCEEDED and streamed a full AI answer (Korean explanation of Chinese national ID format). When clicking on the BLOCKED request (#1) and viewing Response tab, DevTools reports: 'Failed to load response data ??No data found for resource with given identifier'. This is THE key diagnostic: the APF is sending ZERO body bytes to the browser. The stream is terminated (RST_STREAM/GOAWAY) with HEADERS but NO DATA frames. So even though the APF emits 1148B envelope content on the wire, Chrome discards it and reports CORS error because: (a) no valid CORS headers on response, (b) no proper content-type header, (c) stream reset before body completes, or (d) all of the above.
result.warning_text_observed: none (AI answer from retry was rendered successfully ??full Korean explanation of Chinese ID format)
result.key_diagnostic_finding: Chrome DevTools Response tab shows 'Failed to load response data ??No data found for resource with given identifier' when inspecting the APF-blocked chat? request. This proves the APF envelope IS NOT REACHING the browser's response buffer, despite being 1148B on the wire. Chrome is discarding the response due to protocol error / CORS preflight failure / missing response headers.

request_1_blocked.response_body: NONE - Chrome shows 'Failed to load response data'

request_2_succeeded.response_body: Full qianwen SSE with AI-generated Korean content (Chinese ID format explanation)

result.root_cause_analysis: APF is blocking the first chat request with a stream-level error (RST_STREAM or pseudo-CORS failure) WITHOUT first emitting valid response headers + envelope body. Chrome treats this as a network failure, does NOT expose any partial body to JS. qianwen's fetch() receives a network error, triggers retry logic, and the retry eventually bypasses APF (possibly racing against APF's filter state). Net result: user sees the normal AI answer instead of an APF warning.
result.console_errors_observed: 68+ DevTools warnings, 2 errors (exact error messages not captured via right-click copy ??Chrome UIPI prevented detail pane selection in earlier clicks; diagnostic was ultimately obtained via label-based click)

network_evidence.proof_of_zero_body: Response tab shows 'Failed to load response data' for request #1
network_evidence.retry_logic_triggered: Yes ??qianwen frontend retried with new nonce 6f09105jvnk

notes: This diagnostic SOLVES the mystery of why all previous APF envelope iterations (9 attempts per scenario context: h2_mode 0/1/2, h2_end_stream 0/1/2, Content-Length variants) failed to render a warning. The envelope never makes it into Chrome's response buffer because the stream is reset before body arrival. Fix path: APF must emit valid HTTP/2 response semantics (HEADERS ??DATA ??END_STREAM flag on last DATA) instead of stream-reset-based blocking. Until this is fixed, no amount of envelope format tuning will help. Screenshots captured: devtools_network.png (showing both requests) and devtools_response_failed.png (showing 'Failed to load response data').
